events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need identify component/condition and visible defect cues. Scenario: manufacturing floor, terminations & splices, rough-complete, observed field condition: insufficient torque (thermal signature - IR modality). Reference NEC 110.14. Need decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant? Insufficient torque causes high resistance connections, overheating, fire risk. Component condition installed-defective, non-compliant. Risk high or critical? Thermal signature indicates overheating; rough-complete not in service but IR shows hot spot. Could be critical due to fire/thermal hazard. Need produce JSON with fields. Need include findings: loose connection, insufficient torque, overheating per IR, non-compliant with NEC 110.14. Actions: torque to manufacturer specs, re-terminate, re-inspect with IR, verify wire preparation. Rationale. References NEC 110.14. percent_complete maybe 80. value numeric reading? Not given, leave 0. confidence 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty, sound_source empty. Ensure valid JSON.

events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",

  "findings": [
    "Electrical termination/splice installed with insufficient torque",
    "Elevated thermal signature detected by IR indicating high-resistance connection",
    "Connection is non-compliant with NEC 110.14 and manufacturer torque requirements",
    "Potential fire and equipment damage hazard if energized under load"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize circuit before rework",
    "Remove and re-terminate or re-torque splice/termination per manufacturer specifications",
    "Use calibrated torque screwdriver/wrench and verify proper wire preparation",
    "Re-inspect with infrared thermography after re-energization under load"
  ],
  "rationale": "Insufficient torque on an electrical termination or splice creates a high-resistance connection that generates heat, as evidenced by the IR thermal signature. NEC 110.14 requires terminations to be made with proper torque and compatible materials to ensure safe and reliable operation. Because the defect presents an overheating/fire hazard, the installation fails inspection at the rough-complete stage.",
  "references": ["NEC 110.14"]
}
